name: azure-ai-contentsafety-java
description: Build content moderation applications with Azure AI Content Safety SDK for Java. Use when implementing text/image analysis, blocklist management, or harm detection for hate, violence, sexual content, and self-harm.
capabilities: Analyze text for hate, violence, sexual, and self-harm content categories
Analyze images for harmful visual content with severity scoring
Create and manage custom blocklists for domain-specific terms
Configure output severity levels (0-7 scale for text, 0-6 for images)
Integrate with Azure DefaultAzureCredential for secure authentication
Handle API errors with detailed HTTP response information
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer produced false positives due to misinterpreting Markdown documentation. All 33 external_commands findings are Java code examples in Markdown fences, not shell execution. Network findings are example URLs for configuration. Environment variable access is standard Azure SDK pattern. No actual security issues detected.
valueStatement: Moderate harmful content automatically in your applications. Azure AI Content Safety SDK for Java detects hate speech, violence, sexual content, and self-harm with configurable severity thresholds.
userTitle: Build content moderation apps with Azure AI
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and Content Safety resource provisioning
Blocklist changes take approximately 5 minutes to propagate
API calls incur Azure service costs based on volume
Image analysis requires image data or accessible blob URL

useCases.title: Social Platform Moderation
useCases.description: Automatically screen user-generated posts and comments for harmful content before publication. Configure severity thresholds to match community guidelines.

useCases.title: Customer Support Filtering
useCases.description: Detect abusive messages in support tickets and chat systems. Route flagged content to human reviewers while allowing legitimate messages through.

useCases.title: Marketplace Content Review
useCases.description: Scan product listings, reviews, and images for policy violations. Maintain custom blocklists for brand-specific prohibited terms.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Text Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this text for harmful content using Azure AI Content Safety: [paste text]. Return severity scores for each category.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick content check for a single text input

promptTemplates.title: Configured Category Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze [text] for hate and violence categories only. Use 8 severity levels output. Stop if blocklist hit occurs.
promptTemplates.scenario: Targeted analysis with specific categories and blocklist enforcement

promptTemplates.title: Image Moderation from URL
promptTemplates.prompt: Check the image at [URL] for harmful content. Report categories with severity >= 4. Format results as a moderation decision.
promptTemplates.scenario: Remote image analysis with threshold-based filtering

promptTemplates.title: Blocklist Management Workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a blocklist named [name] with description [desc]. Add these terms: [terms]. Then analyze [text] against this blocklist with halt on hit enabled.
promptTemplates.scenario: Complete workflow from blocklist creation to enforced analysis

bestPractices: Read Azure SDK documentation for authentication and client configuration before implementation
Set severity thresholds based on your risk tolerance - severity 4+ is typical for strict moderation
Allow 5 minutes for blocklist changes to propagate before testing new entries
Cache analysis results for repeated content to reduce API calls and latency
antiPatterns: Hardcoding credentials instead of using environment variables or DefaultAzureCredential
Requesting all harm categories when only specific ones are needed - increases latency and cost
Blocking severity 0 content - this creates false positives for borderline cases
Synchronous API calls in high-throughput scenarios - use async patterns for batch processing

faq.question: What Azure subscription do I need for Content Safety?
faq.answer: You need an Azure subscription with a Content Safety resource provisioned. Free tier includes 5,000 transactions per month for testing.

faq.question: How do I authenticate the SDK?
faq.answer: Use either API key authentication with KeyCredential or Azure AD authentication with DefaultAzureCredential for production environments.

faq.question: Can I customize harm detection thresholds?
faq.answer: Yes, set outputType to 8 severity levels and apply your own threshold filtering. The SDK returns numeric severity scores you can evaluate.

faq.question: How quickly do blocklist updates take effect?
faq.answer: Blocklist changes typically propagate within 5 minutes. Plan accordingly when adding newly discovered problematic terms.

faq.question: Does the SDK support async operations?
faq.answer: Yes, the Azure SDK supports asynchronous patterns. Use async client methods for non-blocking operations in high-throughput applications.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The API accepts common image formats including PNG, JPEG, and BMP. Images can be provided as binary data or accessible blob URLs.
